name: Reverse-proxy / Service Cutover Planning Gate
goal: Define a separately approved Caddy/service read-only routing plan after the successful 19.6 short soak and 19.7 bounded extended Python-switch soak, while preserving Python as the rollback and owner for writes/files/migrations.

allowed: reverse proxy protected by external auth
exposure_boundary.auth_note: Prism still has no built-in API token, Bearer token, or user auth layer; any broader exposure must be protected outside Prism before routing changes are considered.

python_service.name: prism.service
python_service.role: primary runtime, write/file/maintenance/migration owner, rollback target

go_sidecar_service.name: prism-go-readonly.service
go_sidecar_service.role: read-only sidecar for validated GET surface only

reverse_proxy.name: Caddy
reverse_proxy.plan: Only route the validated GET read surface to the localhost Go sidecar; all other API, frontend, files, server maintenance, imports/exports, cleanup, and write methods remain on Python.

python_required: POST / PUT / DELETE / PATCH on every path

caddy_plan_shape.config_change_type: separate reversible snippet or clearly bounded route block
caddy_plan_shape.rollback_source: previous Caddy config or disabled snippet
rollback_commands: restore previous Caddy route config or disable Go read route snippet
verify representative GET reads work through Python without X-Prism-Go-Read-Routing

preflight_required_before_19_9_execution: Confirm explicit user approval for live 19.9 execution
Confirm Python prism.service is active and healthy
Confirm Caddy is active and current config can validate before changes
Capture current Caddy config backup or rollback copy
Confirm /api/system/go-read-routing reports enabled=false before Caddy-level routing
Create and verify a fresh timestamped production DB backup
Confirm Go sidecar starts on localhost 127.0.0.1:5002 and /healthz reports schema_version >= 16 plus sqlite_query_only=true
Confirm Go sidecar has no public bind
Confirm all Python-owned routes remain Python-routed before and after the change
Define exact soak duration, sample interval, and rollback trigger before reload
monitoring_evidence_required_for_19_9: GET /api/system/migration-status status and absence of X-Prism-Go-Read-Routing header
POST method check status and absence of X-Prism-Go-Read-Routing header
Go sidecar journal showing no POST/PUT/DELETE/PATCH routed activity
Caddy logs or access evidence sufficient to confirm route ownership
success_criteria: Go sidecar serves only localhost read-only GET surface
Allowed GET read surface returns expected status and carries X-Prism-Go-Read-Routing during the approved live drill
Python-owned routes do not carry X-Prism-Go-Read-Routing
Rollback returns all representative reads to Python-only behavior
No frontend default target, public exposure, write/file/maintenance route, migration, or DB write ownership changes
failure_criteria: Go sidecar binds anything other than localhost
Any public exposure boundary is widened without external auth
Rollback cannot return to Python-only behavior immediately

primary: Disable or remove the Caddy Go read route snippet
Stop prism-go-readonly.service if it is no longer needed
Verify no 127.0.0.1:5002 listener remains if sidecar is stopped
hard_rollback: Do not let Go perform DB repair or migration

result.conclusion: 19.8 closes the reverse-proxy/service cutover planning gap, but it does not authorize live Caddy or frontend routing changes.

allowed_next_step.title: Approved Caddy Read-only Routing Drill
allowed_next_step.scope: Execute a short, reversible Caddy-level read-only routing drill using the 19.8 plan, with fresh backup, caddy validate, live evidence, and rollback to Python-only.
